meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. Use this intermediate checklist to build a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your career.
content.answer_block: To bridge the gap between theory and employment, you must build a production-grade SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform abstract coding skills into a tangible, scalable portfolio piece that proves you can ship real-world software independently.
content.intro:
The 'no portfolio' trap is the silent killer of junior engineering careers. You spend thousands of hours on tutorials, yet your GitHub remains a graveyard of disconnected scripts and unfinished exercises. Employers don't care about your certificates; they care about your ability to solve complex, real-world business problems.

It is time to stop being a student and start being an architect. By building a robust, modular SaaS starter kit, you create a foundation that demonstrates your grasp of authentication, database schema design, and API integration. This isn't just another project—it is your ticket to proving you have the professional velocity required to survive in today's high-stakes AI-driven engineering landscape.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Stack
steps.description: Define the foundational technologies of your SaaS. Focus on a modern stack like Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS to ensure high market relevance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS project architecture using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/db, and Tailwind CSS. Include a folder structure optimized for feature-based development.

steps.title: Implementing Robust Authentication
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Build a secure user flow that handles sign-ups, password resets, and session management using professional standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure authentication middleware for Next.js that protects dashboard routes and handles session persistence with Supabase Auth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement protected routes with auth middleware'

steps.title: Designing the Database Schema
steps.description: Build a relational schema that reflects real-world SaaS requirements, including multi-tenancy and subscription billing models.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS app including tables for profiles, organizations, subscriptions, and usage logs with row-level security policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply RLS policies to profiles and organizations tables'

steps.title: Integrating AI-Powered Features
steps.description: Inject AI functionality into your app to solve a specific user problem, demonstrating your ability to work with LLM APIs effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API route that interacts with the OpenAI API to summarize user-provided text, including error handling and rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add AI summary service with rate limiting'

steps.title: Automating Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it is not live. Configure CI/CD to automate testing and production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment of a Next.js application to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure CI/CD workflow for Vercel'

pro_tips: Always implement Row-Level Security (RLS) from day one; it proves you understand data privacy better than most juniors.
Document your architectural decisions in a 'Design Docs' folder; employers prioritize engineers who can articulate the 'why' behind their code.
Use feature-flagging early to demonstrate how you handle production rollouts without breaking the user experience.
Write unit tests for your core business logic using Vitest; it shows you prioritize reliability over speed.
Add a 'Changelog' file in your repo; it tracks your development journey and demonstrates consistent, long-term commitment.
content.next_steps: Deploy your starter kit to a custom domain, add a landing page that highlights the specific problem your SaaS solves, and link the repository prominently on your LinkedIn and personal portfolio site.
content.conversion_block: Stop building tutorials and start building a career. Your first SaaS starter kit is the bridge between 'aspiring' and 'hired'. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
